records.ety: [OE. chalenge claim, accusation, challenge, OF. chalenge, chalonge, claim, accusation, contest, fr. L. calumnia false accusation, chicanery. See Calumny.]

defs: An invitation to engage in a contest or controversy of any kind; a defiance; specifically, a summons to fight a duel; also, the letter or message conveying the summons.
The act of a sentry in halting any one who appears at his post, and demanding the countersign.

quotes: There must be no challenge of superiority.

defs: The opening and crying of hounds at first finding the scent of their game.

An exception to a juror or to a member of a court martial, coupled with a demand that he should be held incompetent to act; the claim of a party that a certain person or persons shall not sit in trial upon him or his cause.
An exception to a person as not legally qualified to vote. The challenge must be made when the ballot is offered.

records.ety: [OE. chalengen to accuse, claim, OF. chalengier, chalongier, to claim, accuse, dispute, fr. L. calumniar to attack with false accusations. See Challenge, n., and cf. Calumniate.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Challenged (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Challenging.]

defs: To call to a contest of any kind; to call to answer; to defy.
quotes: I challenge any man to make any pretense to power by right of fatherhood.

defs: To call, invite, or summon to answer for an offense by personal combat.
quotes: By this I challenge him to single fight.

defs: To claim as due; to demand as a right.

quotes: He complained of the emperors . . . and challenged them for that he had no greater revenues . . . from them.

defs: To question or demand the countersign from (one who attempts to pass the lines); as, the sentinel challenged us, with “Who comes there?”
To take exception to; question; as, to challenge the accuracy of a statement or of a quotation.
To object to or take exception to, as to a juror, or member of a court.
To object to the reception of the vote of, as on the ground that the person in not qualified as a voter.
To assert a right; to claim a place.
quotes: Where nature doth with merit challenge.
